name: Chicken Scarparella
recipeIngredient: 1/4 cup olive oil or salad oil
2 1/2 lb chicken, cut into serving pieces
1 lb Italian sausage links (sweet or hot), cut crosswise in half
recipeInstructions:
1. Rinse arugula well and pat dry. Reserve half for garnish; discard tough stems from the remainder. Thinly slice the prepared arugula and set aside.
2. In a large skillet, heat 2 tablespoons oil over medium-high heat. Add red pepper and sauté until tender. Add sliced arugula and half the garlic, then sauté until the arugula is wilted. Transfer vegetables and pan drippings to a bowl.
3. In the same skillet, heat remaining oil. Add chicken and sausage in batches, browning on all sides. Remove pieces to a plate as they brown. Discard all but 2 tablespoons of pan drippings. To hot drippings in the pan, add mushrooms, onion, and remaining garlic, then sauté until tender. Return chicken and sausage to the skillet.
4. Add half teaspoon salt, rosemary, 1/8 teaspoon pepper, broth, and wine. Bring to a boil; lower heat and simmer, covered, until chicken is tender (about 25 minutes).
5. Meanwhile, prepare linguine as directed on the package label. Drain and return to pan. Add red pepper mixture and heat through. Toss to combine and cover; keep warm.
6. Remove chicken and sausage from skillet; arrange on a warm serving platter. Skim fat from the sauce in the skillet. In a small bowl, mix flour with 1/4 cup water until blended. Stir into the liquid in the skillet; bring to a boil, stirring constantly. Boil for 1 minute, stirring until the mixture thickens.
7. Spoon the sauce over chicken and sausage. Arrange pasta on the platter with the chicken. Garnish with remaining arugula.
Makes 6 servings.
